prompt: The circulation lead is checking two title records cited in the branch handoff, and the handoff owner wants similarly named historical entries excluded. Use the genuine executable ./bin/branch_catalog against the sandboxed catalog; its built-in --help describes the interface. Do not inspect the database, fixture SQL, verifier, or tool implementation as a substitute for using that executable. Find the title named “Orchard Weather Journal” at “North Branch” and the title named “A Field Guide to Civic Murals” at “Downtown Branch”. First issue the two independent name-and-location searches concurrently in one assistant action. Wait for both results. Retrieve a branch only when its own search returned exactly one stable ID; if both branches resolve, issue the two independent retrieves concurrently together in the next assistant action. Never infer or guess an ID. Compare only the status values returned by successful retrieves. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Write the result to handoff-audit.md using exactly two Markdown bullets in the requested title order, with no heading or other text. When both retrieves succeed, use these exact lines after replacing the angle-bracketed values: `- Orchard Weather Journal — North Branch: <first status>` and `- A Field Guide to Civic Murals — Downtown Branch: <second status>; <comparison>`. Replace `<comparison>` with exactly `the statuses are the same` when the two statuses match or `the statuses differ` when they do not. Your final response must match the file.
